title: /bin/bash: cannot execute binary file
body:
Generated Image seems to be corrupt.

```sh
ctr=$(buildah from centos:7)
buildah config --workingdir /tmp $ctr
buildah config --entrypoint /bin/bash $ctr
buildah commit $ctr test1

podman run --rm -it test1
/bin/bash: /bin/bash: cannot execute binary file
```
A simple example like this works fine in docker:
```Dockerfile
FROM centos:7
ENTRYPOINT /bin/bash
WORKDIR /tmp
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**
```
buildah-0.16-1.git82529cd.fc27.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**
```
Version:       0.16
Go Version:    go1.9.4
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    82529cd
Built:         Wed Apr  4 11:22:14 2018
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**
```
Fedora release 27 (Twenty Seven)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**
```
Linux jw-laptop 4.15.16-300.fc27.x86_64 #1 SMP Mon Apr 9 17:50:06 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary Read/Write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional Read/Only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers.
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body:
This looks like it's related to this pull request / change in entrypoint behaviour that I described in this issue:
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/issues/564

And resolved in this PR:
https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah/pull/577

It looks like podman is passing /bin/bash in to execute and because the entrypoint is set it is being passed as the first parameter to the entrypoint (/bin/bash).

This entrypoint behaviour now matches docker as I noted in the PR. Is podman _always_ passing /bin/bash to the execute? ignoring the configured entrypoint?

-edit-

@jwendell can you execute the podman command with debug to get some more details?

@pixdrift That's probably part of the issue, but if I try running this without Podman using only Buildah, I'm running into some exec/priv errors too.

Interestingly if I use the Dockerfile that @jwendell provided and use it with `buildah bud`, it works fine just as it does for Docker.

```

STEP 2: ENTRYPOINT /bin/bash
STEP 3: WORKDIR /tmp
STEP 4: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.override_kernel_check=true]docker.io/library/testy:latest

93ac0007c439c89029758a6e20f5e1e8b4c56cf03c03fca9bedc868a1002f3b6

testy-working-container

[root@mrsdalloway tmp]# 
```
FYI @mheon and @baude

@pixdrift I don't think this is on the Podman side. Even manually overriding entrypoint, I get the same `cannot execute binary file` error.

@jwendell thanks for the debug
@mheon ok, cheers.
@pixdrift, yes I meant `buildah config --entrypoint` not commit in my prior reply.  Regardless of which I mean to talk about, my fingers invariably type the other.  Sigh.

There is a difference in resulting Entrypoint.

When building using buildah native:
```
            "WorkingDir": "/tmp",
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/bash"
            ],
```

When building using `buildah bud`
```
            "WorkingDir": "/tmp",
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/bin/bash"
            ],
```
  
  

If you modify the buildah native file so that it produces the same `Entrypoint` as the Dockerfile, podman executes as expected.
```
ctr=$(buildah from centos:7)
buildah config --workingdir /tmp $ctr
buildah config --entrypoint "/bin/sh -c /bin/bash" $ctr
buildah commit $ctr pixtest
```

Entrypoint from inspect:
```
            "WorkingDir": "/tmp",
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/bin/bash"
            ],
```

Podman behaviour:
```
# podman run --rm -it pixtest
[root@a2a13f6ec2dd tmp]#
```

Just built the same docker file using `docker build` and it matches the `buildah bud` Entrypoint, so native buildah is different.

From `docker build` docker version 1.13.1:
```
            "WorkingDir": "/tmp",
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/bin/bash"
            ],
```

I still think `/bin/bash` is being passed in (although it's the same behaviour with docker run). Consider the following:
```
# /bin/sh -c /bin/bash /bin/bash
# exit
# /bin/bash /bin/bash
/bin/bash: /bin/bash: cannot execute binary file
```
It's just `/bin/sh -c` handles it gracefully so the end user doesn't see it.


-edit-

Think I have found it, buildah native is always injecting the following, regardless:
```
            "Cmd": [
                "/bin/bash"
            ],
```

buildah bud doesn't do this:
`"Cmd": null,`

If you explicitly force `cmd` to empty in the buildah file, it resolves the issue:
```
#!/bin/bash
ctr=$(buildah from centos:7)
buildah config --entrypoint "/bin/bash" $ctr
buildah config --cmd "" $ctr
buildah commit $ctr pixtest
```
```
# podman run --rm -it pixtest
[root@a759b97b23e9 /]# exit
```

Thinking this is a default in a template somewhere that doesn't get cleaned up. Can't find where this is sourced in the code.

So entrypoint just extends the `sh -c` command.
@TomSweeneyRedHat Can you quickly fix this.
@rhatdan will hop on it this afternoon.
@pixdrift can you open a PR to fix that behaviour.
@rhatdan ack.
I've created #600 to track the issue.
@jwendell, can you re-test with current code?, original issue should be resolved.
Closing reopen if you disagree.

I have found a lingering issue with `entrypoint` behaviour when using `buildah config --entrypoint`

`buildah bud` behaviour appears to match `docker build` behaviour, but `config --entrypoint` results in an image that doesn't correctly receive parameters that are passed to the image.

### To reproduce:

**buildah bud**
Dockerfile:
```
FROM centos
ADD showparams.sh /
ENTRYPOINT [ "/showparams.sh" ]
```

Contents of showparams.sh
```
#!/bin/bash
echo $@
```

Resulting image pushed to docker-daemon and executed with docker
```
docker run entrypoint:buildahbud param1 param2
param1 param2
```

Checking entrypoint with inspect  (relevant values):
```
docker inspect entrypoint:buildahbud
        "Config": {
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/showparams.sh"
            ],
```

**buildah raw**
```
buildah from centos
buildah mount centos-working-container
cp showparams.sh /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/bd0e8eb50125e9a448af6757bf518f4e69854ad1ba08bfc52443093908d52893/merged
buildah umount centos-working-container
buildah config --entrypoint /showparams.sh centos-working-container
buildah commit centos-working-container entrypoint:buildahraw
```

Resulting image pushed to docker-daemon and executed with docker
```
docker run entrypoint:buildahraw param1 param2
<blank line>
```

podman also fails for this example
```
podman run entrypoint:buildahraw param1 param2
<blank line>
```

Checking entrypoint with inspect (relevant values):
```
docker inspect entrypoint:buildahraw
        "Config": {
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/showparams.sh"
            ],
```

**docker build (using same dockerfile as above)**
```
# docker build showparams
Sending build context to Docker daemon  5.12 kB
Step 1/3 : FROM centos
 ---> e934aafc2206
Step 2/3 : ADD showparams.sh /
 ---> 13ea28206ac2
Removing intermediate container 8f50f90e68fc
Step 3/3 : ENTRYPOINT /showparams.sh
 ---> Running in 1747276dff83
 ---> 3c758ec01042
Removing intermediate container 1747276dff83
Successfully built 3c758ec01042
```

Checking entrypoint with inspect (relevant values):
```
# docker inspect 3c758ec01042
        "ContainerConfig": {
            "Cmd": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "#(nop) ",
                "ENTRYPOINT [\"/showparams.sh\"]"
            ],
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/showparams.sh"
            ],


        "Config": {
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/showparams.sh"
            ],
```

As discussed @TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan
I believe #596 may need to be reverted/reviewed.

Created PR #730 which is a revert of sorts (integrates some of the code changes of the last change).

I have tested this with the above scenarios, and also confirmed that @jwendell's example that created this initial issue works correctly with this build.

-edit-

Found an issue here based on how ENTRYPOINT in the Dockerfile is declared.

The following is how `docker build` handles it (and `buildah bud` after testing):

If `ENTRYPOINT` is declared as an array:
```
FROM centos
ADD showparams.sh /
ENTRYPOINT [ "/showparams.sh" ]
```

Results in:
```
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/showparams.sh"
            ],
```

If `ENTRYPOINT` is declared as a string:
```
FROM centos
ADD showparams.sh /
ENTRYPOINT /showparams.sh
```

Results in:
```
            "Entrypoint": [
                "/bin/sh",
                "-c",
                "/showparams.sh"
            ],
```

If you use a string for ENTRYPOINT in either `docker build` or `buildah bud`, the resulting container doesn't print parameters.. which is why changing the `buildah config --entrypoint` behaviour gets a working result.

The two forms are discussed here:
https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#entrypoint

@TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan, any thoughts on how `buildah config --entrypoint` should handle this? Can we pass an array to this?
Closing this issue out, as it was determined entrypoint issue with array/string forms was unrelated to this original issue. Apologies for the noise.
